question: Juan and LaKeisha roll a few objects down a ramp. They want to see which object rolls the farthest. What should they do so they can repeat their investigation?
choices: Change the height of the ramp.
Record the details of the investigation.

question: High-pressure systems stop air from rising into the colder regions of the atmosphere where water can condense. What will most likely result if a high-pressure system remains in an area for a long period of time?
Which characteristic of a cheetah is more likely to be learned rather than inherited?
Plants and animals need food for growth. What happens to most of the food that plants produce?
choices: Food is stored for future use.
Food is used to absorb sunlight.

question: Janette conducts an investigation to see which foods make her feel more fatigued. She eats one of four different foods each day at the same time for four days and then records how she feels. She asks her friend Carmen to do the same investigation to see if she gets similar results. Which would make the investigation most difficult to replicate?
choices: making sure the same foods are eaten
recording observations in the same chart
making sure the foods are at the same temperature

question: Which action does not represent a safety procedure in the lab?
choices: using heat-resistant gloves if glassware is hot
washing a chemical splash with lots of water

Cameron is shopping for a new desk. He wants to make sure the desk will fit in the corner of his room. When Cameron shops for the new desk, which of the following will best help him make sure that the desk will fit?
choices: a picture showing the carpet in his room
a diagram of the measurements of his room
a drawing showing how to put a desk together
a list of tools he needs to put the desk together

question: What does photosynthesis produce that helps plants grow?

Mosquito fish found on the islands of the Bahamas live in various isolated freshwater ponds that were once a single body of water. When several male and female mosquito fish are taken from two isolated ponds and placed into a single pond, the breeding preference of each mosquito fish is for fish from its own original pond. Which of these most likely resulted in this breeding preference?
choices: Availability of food influenced the breeding preferences of the fish.
Competition for a suitable mate influenced the breeding preferences.
Predators in the pond influenced the breeding preferences of the fish.
Speciation due to reproductive isolation influenced the breeding preferences.

question: Students are investigating how a doorbell works. Which is most likely an observation made during the investigation?
choices: A battery is the best power source for a doorbell.
Most people prefer to use a doorbell over knocking.
The doorbell uses a piece of wire wrapped around a magnet.

question: Which of the following best describes the mass of a solid block of ice?
choices: the amount of matter in the block
the amount of space the block takes up
the force of gravity acting on the block
the distance between the molecules in the block

question: An experiment is performed with a sealed container. The container starts out with a temperature of 70 degrees F and an air pressure of 20 psi. The container is heated, and the pressure inside the container rises evenly with increased temperature. If the container is cooled back to 70 degrees F, what will the air pressure be?
Which of these objects will most likely float in water?
Most of the oxygen in the atmosphere is made by algae. Where do most of the algae live?
